content.problemStatement:
hErd gives you an integer NNN. Find any three **positive** integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C such that:


N=lcm(A,B)+lcm(B,C)+lcm(C,A)N = lcm(A,B) + lcm(B,C) + lcm(C,A)N=lcm(A,B)+lcm(B,C)+lcm(C,A); where lcmlcmlcm denotes the least common multiple.
If there is no solution, print −1-1−1.

If there are multiple solutions, you may print any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, three space-separated integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C satisfying the condition.


If there is no solution, print −1-1−1.

If there are multiple solutions, you may print any of them.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output either "-1" (without quotes) if no suitable triple exists, or three space‑separated positive integers A B C such that N = lcm(A,B) + lcm(B,C) + lcm(C,A). Each test case's answer must be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=1 is not divisible by 3, so no solution exists.\nTest case 2: N=6 ⇒ x=2, output (2,2,2). lcm(2,2)=2, sum=3*2=6.\nTest case 3: N=15 ⇒ x=5, output (5,5,5). Each lcm is 5, sum=15.

solution.approach: The sum of three LCMs is at least 3, because each LCM of two positive integers is at least 1. The simplest way to control the sum is to make all three numbers equal: let A = B = C = x. Then lcm(A,B) = lcm(B,C) = lcm(C,A) = x and the required sum becomes 3·x. Therefore a solution exists iff N is a multiple of 3. In that case we can choose x = N / 3 and output (x, x, x). If N is not divisible by 3, no triple of positive integers can give the sum N, so we output -1. This construction is optimal and runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
